---
title: "June 26: Microsimulation Modeling"
---

## Learning objectives

By the end of Day 2, participants should be able to:

- Explain why and when microsimulation is useful.
- Simulate individual patient trajectories.
- Incorporate heterogeneity and patient history.
- Store and summarize patient-level outcomes.
- Compare microsimulation outputs with cohort model outputs.

## Suggested structure

| Session | Topic | Materials |
|---|---|---|
| Morning 1 | Why microsimulation? | Slides, examples |
| Morning 2 | First patient-level simulation | Code demo |
| Afternoon 1 | Heterogeneity, memory, and time dependency | Slides, code demo |
| Afternoon 2 | Microsimulation coding lab | Exercise 3 |
| Homework | Extend the microsimulation model | Homework 2 |

## Files

- [Microsimulation code](../code/day2/03-microsimulation.R)
- [Exercise 3](../exercises/exercise-03-microsimulation.qmd)
